channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK government denies entry to controversial anti-Islam activist over public order concerns
item.description: The UK Home Office has barred US-based influencer Valentina Gomez from entering the country, citing concerns that her presence would not be conducive to the public good. Gomez, who has previously run ...

item.title: The Tragic Death of Bikram Lama: A Homeless Man Lying Unnoticed for Days in Sydney’s St James Tunnel
item.description: Bikram Lama, known as the ‘birdman’ of St James tunnel in Sydney, tragically died after spending days unnoticed in one of the busiest areas of the city. Lama, a former international student from Nepal...

item.title: Hollywood Films About Rescue Missions Amidst US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The recent escalations in tensions between the United States and Iran have sparked a surge in public interest regarding rescue missions and geopolitical conflicts. The original headline, intended to g...

item.title: DepEd Expands Summer Programs to Help Students Improve Learning Skills
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) is set to implement a wider range of summer remediation programs from May 6 to June 3, 2026, aiming to assist learners who are at risk of lagging behind. Education ...

item.title: Ondo Commissioner Faces Scrutiny Over Police Investigation, Alleged Misuse of State Funds
item.description: The Ondo State Commissioner for Finance, Omowumi Isaac, has sparked controversy after being seen supervising what many describe as 'wasteful and questionable' state-funded projects. This comes after s...

item.title: Clash at Gurdwara in Germany Leaves 11 Injured Amid Disputes over Management
item.description: A violent clash at a gurdwara in Moers, Germany, resulted in 11 people being injured on Sunday. The altercation, which involved more than 40 individuals, was triggered by a disagreement over the manag...

item.title: House Republicans Block Extension of Warrantless Surveillance Program
item.description: In a late-night vote on Friday, a group of House Republicans defied party leadership and President Donald Trump by blocking a proposal to extend Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Ac...

item.title: 100 Thoughtful Good Morning Messages to Strengthen Your Relationship
item.description: This article shares a collection of 100 good morning messages designed to help people express love and appreciation to their partners, especially women. The main idea be say small, consistent acts of ...

item.title: Singer D4VD reportedly charged in connection with death of 14-year-old found in vehicle

item.title: Kenya Opens New Legal Aid Centre in Nakuru to Support Prisoners and Vulnerable Communities
item.description: The government of Kenya has launched its fourth legal aid service centre, located at the Nakuru GK Command, aimed at improving access to justice for prisoners and marginalised communities. Attorney Ge...

item.title: Study Shows Nasal Spray Reduces Brain Inflammation and Supports Memory in Mice
item.description: A recent study explores a promising approach to addressing age-related brain decline using a nasal spray. Researchers found that tiny particles released by stem cells, delivered through the nose, can ...

item.title: Global Markets React to Potential US-Iran Talks Amid Uncertainty
item.description: Global markets are experiencing heightened uncertainty as investors await developments in the ongoing negotiations between the United States and Iran. Reports earlier this week suggested Iran might re...

item.title: Key Global Updates: FBI Lawsuit, UK Political Error, Libya Migrant Tragedy and Other Major Developments

item.title: 6 individuals nabbed for illegal petroleum product transfer in Caloocan
item.description: Six individuals were arrested in Caloocan for illegally handling and transferring petroleum products. The operation, conducted by local authorities, uncovered a syndicate engaged in the unauthorized m...

item.title: Six-year-old boy dies in Ellesmere Port after car collision; two men arrested
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Ellesmere Port when a six-year-old boy was fatally struck by a black Infiniti FX30D on Anchor Drive at approximately 11:40 BST on Sunday. Emergency services attended the ...

item.title: Residents of Fort Coulonge Brace for Flooding Following Emergency Declaration
item.description: Residents of Fort Coulonge, Quebec, are preparing for significant flooding after provincial authorities declared a state of emergency. Rising water levels in local rivers, exacerbated by recent heavy ...

item.title: Pakistan secures second $1bn tranche from Saudi deposit amid external financing pressures
item.description: Pakistan has received $1 billion from the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia as part of a previously agreed $3 billion deposit, according to the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP). This amount represents the second tr...

item.title: Farrer by-election candidates focus on water issues amid political tensions
item.description: Around 100 voters gathered at the Exies Sports Club in Griffith, NSW, to hear nine candidates contesting the upcoming Farrer federal by-election, following Sussan Ley's 25-year tenure. The event highl...

item.title: Falak Javed Gets Bail in Azma Bokhari Video Case Following Court Decisions
item.description: Lahore High Court has granted post-arrest bail to PTI social media activist Falak Javed in the ongoing case concerning the alleged circulation of a fake video featuring Punjab Information Minister Azm...

item.title: ADC Youths Protest INEC, Call for Amupitan’s Resignation Amid Party Leadership Crisis
item.description: Youths from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) waka go the Lagos office of Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) for Yaba to protest. Dem dey demand say the INEC chairman, Joash Amupitan...
